PLTFS' OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED WITH DEFTS' RESPONSE TO ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FILED BY PLAINTIFF CASEY FAMILY TRUST CASEY, RICHARD CO-TRUSTEES OF THE CASEY FAMILY TRUST CASEY, GLORIA J CO-TRUSTEES OF THE CASEY FAMILY TRUST

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS CASEY SECURITIES, LLC, GEORGE GASPARININ & JOHN FITZPATRICK'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION IS DENIED. THE DISSOLUTION CLAIM IS NOT SUBJECT TO ARBITRATION BECAUSE IT IS A SEPARATE LEGAL MECHANISM AND DOES NOT ARISE OUT OF OR RELATE TO THE OPERATING AGREEMENT. PLAINTIFF'S CITATIONS TO GEORGIA AND MONTANA LAW ARE PERSUASIVE. THE COURT DENIES FINRA ARBITRATION BECAUSE THE DISSOLUTION DOES NOT ARISE OUT OF FINRA BUSINESS ACTIVITIES. THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANTS' ESTOPPEL ARGUMENT. PLAINTIFF NEVER AGREED TO SUBMIT THE DISSOLUTION ISSUE TO ARBITRATION. THE FRAUDULENT TRANSFER CLAIM IS DIRECTLY RELATED TO THE DISSOLUTION AND THEREFORE, IT IS NOT SUBJECT TO ARBITRATION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MTN TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND REQ FOR SANCTIONS FILED BY PLAINTIFF CASEY FAMILY TRUST CASEY, RICHARD CO-TRUSTEES OF THE CASEY FAMILY TRUST CASEY, GLORIA J CO-TRUSTEES OF THE CASEY FAMILY TRUST

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Attendance At Deposition And Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Apr-19-2013 Text Ruling

RULING - A R G U E D: Plaintiffs Casey Family Trust, Richard Case, and Gloria Casey's Motion to Compel Attendance at Deposition and Production of Documents is granted. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Huibonhoa; Clerk L. Fong; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action; Notice Of
Off Calendar Mar-14-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Guta, Esq. of Casey Securities, Tel. 415.852.6493, for defendant and moving party;
Laura C. Roche, Esq. of Farella Braun & Martel, Tel. 415.954.4950, for plaintiffs.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs CASEY SECURITIES, LLC, GEORGE GASPARININ & JOHN FITZPATRICK's Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action is denied. The dissolution claim is not subject to arbitration because it is a separate legal mechanism and does not arise out of or relate to the operating agreement. Plaintiff's citations to Georgia and Montana law are persuasive. The Court denies FINRA arbitration because the dissolution does not arise out of FINRA business activities. The Court rejects defendants' estoppel argument. Plaintiff never agreed to submit the dissolution issue to arbitration. The fraudulent transfer claim is directly related to the dissolution and therefore, it is not subject to arbitration. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Mar-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KELLY WOODRUFF OF FARELLA BRAUN LLP, TEL. NO. 954-4400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. MICHAEL GUTA, TEL. NO. 544-9106 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;

RULING - A R G U E D; Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction - The motion for preliminary injunction is denied without prejudice because the moving papers are based solely on the the verified complaint which is insufficient evidence for the court to issue a restraining order or injunction. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT, Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/AJR
